arms.predicted: For baseline scenario, f(M) exhibits a two-phase structure: concave monotone rise from M=2 to M*=40 where df/dM decreases after M≈8, followed by flat plateau for M>40 with variation < 0.05%. Peak M*=40 coincides exactly with TTFT/ITL crossover (TTFT-bound below, ITL-bound above).
arms.observed: Baseline: 0 monotone violations on rising (M=2..40). Plateau variation = 0.01681% < 0.05% target. Convex onset ends at M=8, post-onset diffs strictly decreasing (post_onset_concave=True). Crossover confirmed exactly at M=40: M=39 TTFT-bound (RPSTargetTTFT=2.1499, RPSTargetITL=2.1670), M=40 ITL-bound (RPSTargetTTFT=2.1856, RPSTargetITL=2.1668). Transition width 90%→99%: 5 steps (M90=34, M99=39). Peak throughput=2.1667778 req/s.

arms.predicted: For short-tight-ttft scenario, RPSTargetITL > RPSTargetTTFT for ALL M in [2, 256]. No crossover. f(M) rises and saturates with M*=69 being noise (< 0.004% above M=34).
arms.observed: RPSTargetITL is 2.3x–2.7x RPSTargetTTFT at all probed M (10, 30, 69, 150, 256) — ITL never binds. Gap between M=34 (2.7530394) and M=69 (2.7530658) = 0.000959% < 0.004% target. 18 violations occur on M=2..69 range, but all 18 are in the flat region (M≥33) where binary-search noise dominates (max violation = 0.00374% < 0.01% threshold). True rising region (M=2..26, i.e. up to 99% of peak) is perfectly monotone.
arms.diagnostic_note: The 18 violations counted on the nominal 'rising' region (M≤69) are entirely in the flat plateau (M≥33 is past the 99%-of-peak threshold M99=26). They represent binary-search numerical noise at the ~0.001% level, not true non-monotonicity.

arms.predicted: All 4 scenarios exhibit two-phase structure. Plateau variation < 0.15% in every scenario. Rising portion monotone (violations < 0.01% of peak). Transition width 4-21 steps. Long-loose-itl has widest transition (≈21 steps).
arms.observed: All 4 scenarios confirmed two-phase structure. Plateau variations: baseline=0.01681%, short-tight-ttft=0.00144%, long-loose-itl=0.02836%, small-queue=0.07263% — all < 0.15%. Max rising violation: baseline=0%, short-tight-ttft=0.00374%, long-loose-itl=0%, small-queue=0% — all < 0.01%. Transition widths: baseline=5, short-tight-ttft=4, long-loose-itl=21, small-queue=4 — all within predicted range [4,21]. Long-loose-itl has exactly the widest transition at 21 steps. Long-loose-itl crossover confirmed at M=93 (M=92 TTFT-bound, M=93 ITL-bound), matching M_truth=93.

discrepancy_analysis: All three arms confirmed within predicted bounds. Key quantitative results: (1) Baseline plateau variation 0.017% vs predicted <0.05%; (2) Short-tight-ttft M=34→M=69 gap 0.00096% vs predicted <0.004%; (3) All 4 scenarios plateau variation <0.15%; (4) Long-loose-itl exactly 21 transition steps as predicted. Only minor discrepancy: short-tight-ttft post-onset concavity (post_onset_concave=False) is not confirmed, but this is expected since its 'rising' region ends at M≈9 and the remainder is a flat plateau where binary-search noise dominates the diffs — concavity is irrelevant in the noise-dominated flat region. The h-main concavity claim applies specifically to baseline and is confirmed.
